subject: Error-Handling Audit —SquadDash WPF Application
body:
## Error-Handling Audit — SquadDash WPF Application

**Tech stack:** C# / .NET, WPF desktop (Windows), `async/await`, `Task.Run` fire-and-forget, `DispatcherTimer`, WebBrowser COM interop.

**Global handler status:** ✅ `App.xaml.cs` correctly registers `DispatcherUnhandledException`, `AppDomain.UnhandledException`, and `TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException`. These provide a safety net but cannot supply call-site context.

---

## Category 1 — Silent Error Suppression

### `MarkdownRevisionExecutor.cs` · `DirectRevise` (fire-and-forget lambda, line ~97)
**Severity: High**

The outer `Task.Run` lambda ends with `catch { /* swallow — user may have navigated away */ }`. This discards every failure — network errors, API rate limits, SDK crashes — with no log entry and no user notification. The working overlay is dismissed (via `finally`) but nothing tells the user that the revision silently failed.

**Recommended fix:** Log at trace level and dispatch a short toast/status message instead of swallowing completely.

---

### `MainWindow.xaml.cs` · `DirectReviseTextBox` (fire-and-forget `Task.Run`, line ~14285)
**Severity: High**

Identical pattern: `catch { /* swallow — user may have navigated away */ }`. Any failure disappears silently. Overlay is removed but user has no idea the AI call failed.

---

### `InboxMessageWindow.cs` + `InboxPanelController.cs` · chip click handlers
**Severity: Medium**

All four attachment-chip interaction cases (`url`, `file`, `task-ref`, `text`) end with empty `catch { }`. The user clicks a link and nothing happens, with no error message.

---

### `MainWindow.xaml.cs` · doc-rename heading update (line ~26414)
**Severity: Medium**

Heading-update write failure is silently swallowed, then the file is renamed anyway — leaving content inconsistent with the new filename.

---

### `MainWindow.xaml.cs` · silent file-read fallbacks (lines ~26120, ~27125, ~27153)
**Severity: Medium**

Three separate `catch { }` blocks silently fall back to empty string when `File.ReadAllText` fails. Callers operate silently on empty content.

---

### `MaintenanceStubStateStore.cs` · `Load` (line ~33)
**Severity: Low**

Bare `catch { }` — `Save()` in the same class correctly traces on failure; `Load()` does not.

---

### `MarkdownDocumentWindow.cs` · `SetupFileWatcher` (line ~2111)
**Severity: Low**

`FileSystemWatcher` construction wrapped in `catch { }`. If watcher setup fails, external file changes are silently ignored for the lifetime of that tab.

---

## Category 2 — Catch-and-Return-Sentinel

### `NativeMethods.cs` · `GetCursorScreenPos` (line ~239)
**Severity: Medium**

Win32 exception becomes `default(Point)` (origin), indistinguishable from a legitimately-positioned cursor at `(0, 0)`. Used for popup placement — silent failure places UI elements at screen origin.

---

### `Screenshots/ScreenshotComparator.cs` · `Compare` (line ~45)
**Severity: Low**

`catch (Exception)` returns a `Skipped` sentinel for any failure. A programming error would silently become a skipped comparison.

---

## Category 3 — UI Event Handlers Without Error Guards

### `DocRevisePopup.cs` · `InstructionBox_PreviewKeyDown` (line ~232)
**Severity: High**

`async void` handler calls `awaitSubmitAsync()` with no try/catch. Any synchronous exception before the fire-and-forget handoff propagates to `DispatcherUnhandledException` with no revision-specific context.

---

### `MainWindow.xaml.cs` · `OnInteractiveCaptureCompleted` (line ~29106)
**Severity: Low**

One-liner `async void` with no guard for synchronous throws before the inner try/catch.

---

## Category 4 — Unguarded Async / Concurrent Entry Points

### `LoopController.cs` · `StartAsync` (line ~88)
**Severity: High**

`_ = Task.Run(() => RunLoopAsync(…))` — any unhandled exception kills the loop silently. `IsRunning` may stay `true`, `_onStopped` is never called, leaving the UI showing the loop as running when it is dead.

---

### `WhisperSpeechRecognitionService.cs` · `StartAsync` (line ~60)
**Severity: Medium**

`_ = ChunkLoopAsync(_cts.Token)` — a flush failure faults the task silently; audio transcription stops with no indication to callers.

---

### `TranscriptConversationManager.cs` · `QueueConversationSave` (line ~1384)
**Severity: Medium**

Fire-and-forget; outer `try/finally` could throw after disposal, faulting the task silently and potentially losing conversation data.

---

### `BackgroundTaskPresenter.cs` · `ScheduleAgentThreadFadeIfNeeded` + `ScheduleBackgroundAgentReportPromotion`
**Severity: Low**

Both fire `_ = Task.Run(async () => { … })`. UI delegate calls after `await Task.Delay` have no surrounding try/catch.

---

## Category 5 — Missing Resource Cleanup on Error Paths

### `MainWindow.xaml.cs` · `MainWindow_Closed` (line ~22412)
**Severity: Low**

`await Task.WhenAll(…)` unwrapped. If any shutdown task faults, partial cleanup may be skipped before `DispatcherUnhandledException` handles it.

---

## Category 6 — Overly Broad Exception Catches

### `LoopController.cs` · `RunLoopAsync` (line ~124)
**Severity: Medium**

All exceptions from the pre-iteration hook are swallowed without logging — a programming error would cause the loop to retry indefinitely with no trace.

---

## Category 7 — Exception Messages Missing Context

### `TranscriptConversationManager.cs` · `ProcessQueuedConversationSavesAsync` (line ~1437)
**Severity: Low**

Log message omits folder path and version number; diagnosing which workspace's data was lost requires cross-referencing earlier trace lines.

---

## Category 9 — Global Error Handler Gaps

✅ **No gaps found.** All three WPF global handlers are correctly registered in `App.xaml.cs`.

---

## Category 10 — Cancellation Signals Swallowed

### `LoopController.cs` · `RunLoopAsync` (line ~151)
**Severity: Low**

Post-abort `catch { }` discards all exception types, including unexpected non-cancellation exceptions from bridge failure during shutdown.

---

## Summary

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| High | 4 |
| Medium | 8 |
| Low | 8 |
| **Total** | **20** |

### Top 3 to Address First

1. **`MarkdownRevisionExecutor.DirectRevise` + `MainWindow.DirectReviseTextBox`** — Revision failures are invisibly swallowed; users get no feedback when the core AI revision feature fails.

2. **`LoopController.StartAsync` fire-and-forget `Task.Run`** — An unhandled exception kills the loop silently; `IsRunning` drifts, `_onStopped` never called, UI shows loop as running when dead.

3. **`DocRevisePopup.InstructionBox_PreviewKeyDown` async void** — Synchronous exception in submit path reaches `DispatcherUnhandledException` with no revision-specific context.

actions.label: Fix revision swallow bugs
actions.prompt:
During an error-handling audit of SquadDash, two related High-severity issues were found where AI-revision failures are silently swallowed with no user feedback:

1. `SquadDash/MarkdownRevisionExecutor.cs` — method `DirectRevise`, inside the `_ = Task.Run(async () => { … })` lambda. Line ~97: `catch { /* swallow — user may have navigated away */ }`. This catch discards all exceptions (network errors, API failures, SDK crashes). The working overlay is dismissed via `finally`, but the user gets no indication the revision failed. Fix: log with `SquadDashTrace.Write` and dispatch a short error notification or populate a `RevisionResultWindow` with an error message.

2. `SquadDash/MainWindow.xaml.cs` — search for `DirectReviseTextBox` or `/* swallow — user may have navigated away */`. Same `_ = Task.Run(async () => { … })` with identical catch. Fix identically.

For both: in the catch block, at minimum call `SquadDashTrace.Write("Revision", $"Revision failed: {ex}")` and use `Dispatcher.Invoke(() => { /* show error to user */ })` to surface a message. The overlay cleanup in `finally` is correct — keep it.

Do not change any other code. Build and verify the project still compiles (`dotnet build SquadDash\SquadDash.csproj`).
actions.hint: Task Arjun to fix the two silent revision-failure catch blocks

actions.label: Fix LoopController fire-and-forget
actions.prompt:
During an error-handling audit of SquadDash, a High-severity issue was found in `SquadDash/LoopController.cs`, method `StartAsync` (line ~88):

`_ = Task.Run(() => RunLoopAsync(config, continuousContext, _cts.Token, resumeFromIteration));`

`RunLoopAsync` has no outer try/catch. Any unhandled exception causes the Task to fault and leaves `IsRunning` as `true` and `_onStopped` never called.

Fix: add a broad exception catch inside `RunLoopAsync` that (1) calls `_onError($"Loop crashed unexpectedly: {ex.Message}")` if not already aborting, (2) logs via `SquadDashTrace.Write("Loop", $"RunLoopAsync unhandled: {ex}")`, (3) ensures `IsRunning = false` is still set (it's in `finally`, so already guaranteed).

Also narrow the `catch (Exception)` for `_onBeforeIteration` failures (line ~124) to log: `SquadDashTrace.Write("Loop", $"_onBeforeIteration threw: {ex.Message}")`.

Do not change any other code. Build and verify: `dotnet build SquadDash\SquadDash.csproj`.
actions.hint: Task Arjun to fix the LoopController fire-and-forget exception handling

actions.label: Dismiss
actions.hint: Acknowledge report — no action will be taken
